title: U.S. Honor Flag to be deployed to Mass. for fallen trooper’s memorial
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The U.S. Honor Flag will be sent to Massachusetts to honor fallen State Trooper Kevin Trainor, killed by a wrong-way driver.
Memorial services include a wake on Tuesday and a funeral Wednesday at St. James Church in Salem, with road closures expected.
The U.S. Honor Flag, famous for flying at Ground Zero post-9/11, will arrive from Tampa and has significant historical ties to Massachusetts.

title: Brookline voters overwhelmingly approve tax override to avoid school, public safety cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brookline voters approved a tax override with nearly 60% support, avoiding cuts to public safety and school programs.
The tax override will raise $23 million over three years, increasing property taxes by 18% instead of the projected 11%.
If not approved, Brookline faced eliminating 240 full-time positions and increasing class sizes in schools.

title: Do the Bruins have a potential No. 1 center already on their roster?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are evaluating their current roster for potential top-line centers, focusing on young forwards Fraser Minten and James Hagens.
Bruins management acknowledges the difficulty in acquiring a true No. 1 center through trades or free agency due to high demand.
Fraser Minten and James Hagens are seen as long-term options for center roles, though significant development is still needed.

title: Maine police arrest victim’s boyfriend for 1987 murder
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Maine police arrested Stephen Bouchard, 63, for the 1987 murder of his girlfriend, Alice Hawkes.
Hawkes was found dead in their Westbrook apartment after Bouchard reported being locked out.
A reinvestigation by the Maine Attorney General's Office led to Bouchard's murder charge in 2025.

title: Is Patriots’ 2026 schedule actually easier than many expected? This win-projection metric says so.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Sharp Football Analysis ranks the Patriots' 2026 schedule as the 12th-easiest in the NFL.
Patriots will face 2025 division champions like the Steelers, Jaguars, and Seahawks next season.
Projected win totals and competition quality suggest an easier schedule despite facing eight playoff teams from 2025.

title: Could Patriots reunite with Stefon Diggs? One Patriots writer doesn’t expect it.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs is seeking new opportunities in the NFL following his release in March.
Patriots reporter Phil Perry doubts Diggs will return to New England, citing potential trade interests in A.J. Brown.
Diggs posted strong stats after his ACL recovery, but financial and strategic considerations led to his release.

title: Man, 31, killed in New Bedford shooting
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Coan, 31, was shot and killed in his car in New Bedford on May 6.
Gunfire was detected by ShotSpotter near Acushnet Avenue and Russell Street at 11:30 p.m.
The incident is under investigation as a homicide by State and New Bedford police.

title: Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kevin Hayden, Rachael Rollins, and Linda Champion secure over 1,000 signatures each to qualify for the Suffolk DA primary ballot.
Rachael Rollins, former Suffolk DA, seeks a comeback following her 2023 resignation due to ethics violations and federal investigations.
The Suffolk District Attorney primary on September 1 will reduce the three candidates to two before the November general election.

title: These are Boston’s highest-paid tech CEOs, BBJ data shows
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Wayfair CEO Niraj Shah earned $280.9 million in total compensation in 2025, topping the list of highest-paid Boston tech CEOs.
Vincent Roche of Analog Devices and Neil Barua of PTC Inc. ranked second and third, earning $27.5M and $24.3M respectively.
BBJ used stock awards, bonuses, and salaries from SEC proxy statements to compile the list of top-paid Boston tech executives.

title: Karen Read’s SUV heads to auction in Woburn. Here’s what readers think it’s worth.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Karen Read's 2021 Lexus LX 570 is auctioned in Woburn, linked to her 2022 murder trial.
Boston.com readers predict auction bids ranging from under $70,000 to over $500,000 for the controversial SUV.
The SUV had a rough storage period but received repairs before the auction, with a market value of $55,180-$63,880.
